rationale.root_cause: They correctly pinpointed line 12 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) and explained argv data can exceed the 256-byte buffer, but they ambiguously called it a 'heap-buffer-overflow (or stack-buffer-overflow)', failing to name it definitively as a stack_buffer_overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed a length check before copying (if (argv > 1 && strlen(argc[1]) < sizeof(buf)) { strcpy(buf, argc[1]); } else { ... }) which rejects overlong input and ensures null-termination via strcpy.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended using bounded string APIs (snprintf/strncpy etc.) and centralizing input validation before copying, which removes raw, unbounded string handling from the data path.
